What's going on everyone? It's Super Bowl Sunday, way in day overview. So this is the overview for how way in day is going to go down. All right, so there are a couple things to take into consideration. So the duration scheduling, how many per time slot days, who is going to be running it. That is going to be the first thing we cover from a logistics standpoint, like which of the different setups that we have tested, you will choose to do. Second, within the actual way in day overview, so this is the first thing that you'll go through, you'll go through the tech, meal plans, stats, recording, what I mean by that is you're going to be writing down their weight, their body fat percentage, recording it, make sure they understand how to use Facebook, kind of check in, assigning them the right meal plans, all that kind of jazz. After that, you're going to explain the nutrition plan that you just gave to them so that they feel like they are very well equipped. You will use the nutrition training that is according to whatever play you are doing. So whether you're using six week or 42 day or detox or whatever, whichever one you're doing, you'll, or if you're doing an internal play, then it would be 20 day, belly fat, whatever it is, like we have a zillion different plays, whatever the nutrition explanation that goes along with that is the one that you will use. But the layout of the presentation will still work exactly the same along with supplements selling secrets so that you will seed the sale for supplements, which comes next. So the nutrition explanation independent of what widget we are fulfilling with is still going to lead into a natural supplement sale. After that, we have a one sentence hook that leads to a food sale, which I'll explain more. And then finally you will do their after pictures. And so their after pictures we purposely put last so that basically so people don't duck out and that way if you want to, like you want to make sure that you finish the sale, no one like jacked out because they feel like they've got everything done and it's just a pitch. So like that way you can hold them there and by switching the way we did this, we sold a lot more people. So I would highly recommend doing that. And just as a reminder for everyone from an overview standpoint, keep the no sale sale at home participants separate. So for those of you who, so let's say you signed up 10 people during the week into your service, so whatever it is, so they actually put a deposit down or they paid for the front end program. And then you had seven people who decided to do the no sale approach. So those no sale people make sure that you schedule those seven people at a separate time. As a reminder again, those ones are not going to have 100% show up rates the way the people who paid will. So they'll probably have the same 50% show rate and you'll need to send a reminder to those people just like a normal appointment. So I would with those ones, I would probably be booking like four per 30 minutes. So if you have seven people, I'd probably do like four at the top of the hour and then the other three at the 30 minute mark. That is 100% your prerogative and just as a reminder, people who do not buy service still spend just much if not more on product. So come into those with intention of closing. And that concludes our way into overview.
